spec_match.rationale: The demo fails to match the spec: the server does not send the required 'Supports-Responsive-Sizing: 1' header for child.html, and the native feature is faked/masked by an unconditional postMessage polyfill in the parent.

interactive.rationale: Provides a live slider that updates the embedded blocks in real-time.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The explanation is correct, though the implementation doesn't actually run it natively.

edge_cases.rationale: Provides a fallback polyfill for unsupported browsers, but executes it unconditionally, masking the server-side header misconfiguration that breaks the native feature in supporting browsers.

design_system.rationale: Adheres to the design system styling.

openQuestions.title: Configure server to send Supports-Responsive-Sizing header
openQuestions.detail: The server (server.ts) must be updated to serve child.html with the 'Supports-Responsive-Sizing: 1' HTTP header. Without this, Chrome 150+ will refuse to natively resize the iframe, making the declarative feature completely non-functional.

openQuestions.title: Make polyfill conditional on native support
openQuestions.detail: The parent index.html page's postMessage event listener should only apply the height update if native responsive sizing is not supported (e.g. check !('allowResponsiveSizing' in HTMLIFrameElement.prototype)). This ensures we are actually testing the browser's native implementation in supporting browsers.
